title: rosa rubiginosa subvar. densa
intro: Rosa rubiginosa subvar. densa, also known as the dwarf sweet-briar, is a compact and fragrant shrub native to Europe and Asia, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant's leaves, flowers, and roots are used in herbal medicine, exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The health benefits of Rosa rubiginosa subvar. densa include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. The plant's herbal preparations include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its benefits.
